sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Zevra Therapeutics Receives EMA Negative Opinion on Arimoclomol Marketing Application
feed.summary: Zevra Therapeutics announced that the European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) issued a negative opinion on its Marketing Authorization Application for arimoclomol, a treatment for Niemann-Pick disease type C (NPC). The company plans to request a re-examination of this decision. While this is a regulatory setback for potential European commercialization, Zevra continues to support eligible patients through its global Expanded Access Program.

A precise extraction would require parsing the HTML image source directly.)

feed.title: GE HealthCare CFO Jay Saccaro steps down
feed.summary: GE HealthCare's CFO, Jay Saccaro, is stepping down from his position to pursue an opportunity outside of the medtech sector, a move that surprised analysts. George Newcomb, Controller and Chief Accounting Officer, will serve as interim CFO. This change comes as GE HealthCare reported preliminary second-quarter results showing revenue growth and adjusted earnings per share above expectations, maintaining its full-year forecast.

feed.title: AbbVie stock may move 4.2% on July 31 earnings report By Investing.com
feed.summary: AbbVie Inc. (NYSE:ABBV) stock is projected to move 4.2% when the pharmaceutical company releases its earnings report on July 31, based on options data. Historically, the stock's actual movement has exceeded the implied move in five out of the last eight earnings announcements. The article details past earnings report movements and implied versus actual stock changes.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive stock may move 2.5% on July 31 earnings
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) stock is projected to move 2.5% when the company releases its earnings report on July 31. This forecast is based on options data compiled by Bloomberg. Analysis of past earnings announcements shows that the actual stock price movement has exceeded the implied move in four of the last eight reports.

feed.title: Micron, onsemi, Seagate, Western Digital, and Vishay Intertechnology Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Micron, onsemi, Seagate, Western Digital, and Vishay Intertechnology experienced a decline following the U.S. government's announcement of new tariffs on 60 trading partners, including key players in the global semiconductor supply chain. These tariffs, ranging from 10% to 12.5%, target regions like the European Union, Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan, which are crucial for specialty chemicals, raw silicon wafers, and fabrication equipment. The market reaction indicates concerns about long-term margin compression for U.S. hardware and semiconductor companies, despite a recent recovery in the sector driven by optimism around AI investments.

feed.title: SLB tops second-quarter forecasts as growth in production systems offsets Middle East weakness
feed.summary: SLB reported strong second-quarter results, exceeding Wall Street expectations in both earnings and revenue, driven by robust international activity and growth in its Production Systems and Digital businesses. Although Middle East disruptions impacted performance, these were offset by strong subsea activity and demand for production technologies. The company's data center business also experienced significant expansion, and SLB maintained solid cash flow and announced a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Cadence Design to Release Q2 Earnings: Here's What to Expect
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems, Inc. (CDNS) is set to release its Q2 2026 earnings on July 27, with analysts expecting a significant increase in EPS and revenues driven by AI-driven semiconductor complexity and broad momentum in EDA, IP, and SDA sectors. Despite an impressive earnings surprise history, Zacks' model does not predict an earnings beat this quarter due to a 0.00% Earnings ESP. The company's focus on Generative AI and strategic acquisitions are highlighted as key growth factors, though U.S.-China tech tensions and competition remain concerns.

feed.title: Will Tariff Lawsuits and July Earnings Shift First Solar's (FSLR) Risk Management Narrative?
feed.summary: First Solar (FSLR) is facing securities class action lawsuits alleging misleading disclosures regarding its handling of U.S. tariff policy and production decisions. The upcoming June-quarter 2026 earnings report on July 30 will be critical in reshaping the investment narrative and clarifying how effectively the company has managed policy and operational risks. The outcome of these legal challenges and the earnings report will significantly influence perceptions of First Solar's ability to profit from U.S. trade rules and incentives without unexpected performance issues.

feed.title: Boston Properties (BXP) Stock Looks Below Fair Value After Asset Sale Plans
feed.summary: Boston Properties (BXP) stock appears to be at a valuation crossroads, with a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model suggesting it is undervalued by 23.4%, while its P/E ratio indicates it is fairly valued compared to a tailored model. The company's recent plan to sell a ground lease for 7 Times Square for over $700 million aims to improve balance sheet flexibility, though ongoing office market uncertainty remains a key risk. Investors are divided, with some highlighting its premier market position and capital recycling, while others focus on funding and interest cost risks amidst broader valuation checks that show a mixed picture.

feed.title: Novo escalates legal fight with Lilly; J&J reinforces ‘dominance’ in multiple myeloma
feed.summary: Novo Nordisk has escalated its legal dispute with Eli Lilly, seeking an injunction to stop allegedly "misleading" obesity drug advertisements. Meanwhile, Johnson & Johnson reported strong results for a multiple myeloma drug combination, Revolution Medicines' pancreatic tumor medicinedaraxonrasib was accepted for FDA review, and AstraZeneca received European approval for a new breast cancer drug combination. Additionally, AI drug discovery firm Formation Bio appointed Michael Ehlers as its new chief scientific officer.

feed.title: American Express Earnings: Revenue Growth Exceeds Our Expectations but Is Offset by Higher Expenses
feed.summary: American Express reported strong second-quarter earnings driven by increased card member spending, with net revenue rising 10% to $19.6 billion. Despite a 12% increase in operating expenses, primarily due to the Platinum card refresh and service rewards, Morningstar has raised its fair value estimate for AXP to $335 per share. Analysts believe the market is overly focused on unchanged EPS guidance rather than the underlying business strength, including a 9.5% rise in billed business.

feed.title: Con Edison offers new bill assistance program to qualifying customers amid extreme heat
feed.summary: Con Edison has launched a new Enhanced Energy Assistance Program (EEAP) to help income-qualified households in New York state manage their energy bills, especially amid extreme summer heat. Over 20,000 customers have already applied, with participants saving upwards of $168 off their monthly bills. The company urges customers facing difficulties to apply for assistance programs, which include substantial discounts for those receiving government aid.

feed.title: General Dynamics Stock And 2 Defense Names Backed By Long Cycle Demand
feed.summary: This article identifies three defense and aerospace stocks—General Dynamics (GD), L3Harris Technologies (LHX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC)—that are positioned to benefit from ongoing long-cycle spending and government-backed budgets. It provides an overview of each company's operations, market cap, and highlights key investment considerations, including backlog, valuation, and potential risks like debt and execution challenges. The goal is to assist investors looking for opportunities in sectors tied to security, aviation, and critical infrastructure.

feed.title: Does Illinois Talc Verdict Successor Liability Ruling Change The Bull Case For Kenvue (KVUE)?
feed.summary: An Illinois Appellate Court upheld a US$45 million verdict against Johnson & Johnson for asbestos-contaminated talc, also confirming Kenvue's successor liability. This ruling reinforces the ongoing legal exposure Kenvue faces due to legacy talc products, even as it pursues a merger with Kimberly Clark. Investors are urged to consider this legal risk alongside Kenvue's financial forecasts and fair value estimates.

feed.title: Tranche Update on Lamb Weston Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:LW)'s Equity Buyback Plan announced on December 20, 2018.
feed.summary: Lamb Weston Holdings, Inc. has provided an update on its equity buyback plan announced in December 2018. Between February 23, 2026, and July 24, 2026, the company repurchased 1,495,118 shares for $63.2 million, completing 1.08% of the plan. Cumulatively, Lamb Weston has repurchased 13,513,891 shares, representing 9.44% of the shares for a total of $880.5 million under the stated buyback program.

feed.title: Facebook Launches Dedicated Seller App As Marketplace Steps Up eBay Competition
feed.summary: Meta has launched a dedicated "Seller" app for Facebook Marketplace, aiming to streamline operations for higher-volume sellers and intensify competition with platforms like eBay. The app offers tools for bulk listing, inventory management, performance tracking, and AI-powered listing suggestions, centralizing features previously spread across different Facebook interfaces. This move signals Meta's intent to evolve Marketplace from a classifieds-like service into a more robust e-commerce platform, despite existing challenges like scams and support issues.

feed.title: More Than 12,000 Seek Compensation Directly Through SCE for Eaton Fire Recovery
feed.summary: Southern California Edison announced that over 12,000 individuals, trusts, and legal entities have sought compensation directly through its Wildfire Recovery Compensation Program following the Eaton Fire. The program has extended more than 2,200 offers totaling over $750 million and paid more than $360 million to over 2,300 claimants. SCE is also addressing common myths about the program, clarifying that claim submissions do not impact litigation rights, donations do not reduce compensation, and the program offers a streamlined path comparable to litigation settlements.

feed.title: What to Expect From Agilent Technologies' Q3 2026 Earnings Report
feed.summary: Agilent Technologies (A) is anticipated to announce its Q3 2026 earnings soon, with analysts projecting an adjusted EPS of $1.48, an 8% increase year-over-year. The company has a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts, with an average price target offering a 13.7% upside. Agilent's shares rose significantly after its strong Q2 results and raised fiscal 2026 guidance.

feed.title: Ross accelerates brick-and-mortar expansion with nearly 50 new locations
feed.summary: Ross Stores has opened 47 new locations across its Ross Dress for Less and DD's Discounts brands in June and July, aiming for a total of approximately 110 new stores in 2026, representing about 5% unit growth. The expansion focuses on strengthening its presence in states like Puerto Rico, New York, Michigan, California, Florida, North Carolina, and Texas. This aggressive expansion comes as off-price retailers continue to gain market share, driven by inflation and cautious consumers, with Ross reporting an "outstanding" first quarter with sales up 21% and net income up almost 36%.

feed.title: Textron to Post Q2 Earnings: What's in the Cards for the Stock?
feed.summary: Textron Inc. (TXT) is scheduled to release its second-quarter 2026 results on July 28, with expectations of increased revenues in its Aviation, Bell, and Textron Systems units due to higher sales volumes and aftermarket services. However, the Industrial segment might see lower sales from specialized vehicles, and the overall bottom line could be impacted by higher costs. While analysts project revenue growth of 2.8%, earnings are expected to decline by 1.9%, and Zacks' model does not conclusively predict an earnings beat for TXT this quarter.

feed.title: Ex-Morgan Stanley Broker Suspended Over Risky Options Strategy, Off-Channel Comms
feed.summary: A former Morgan Stanley broker, Theodore W. Byrer, has been suspended for 14 months by FINRA for recommending unsuitable, speculative options strategies to clients, mismarking trades as unsolicited, and using unapproved text messages for business communications. His actions led to significant client losses and commissions. Byrer also allegedly violated Regulation Best Interest and record-keeping rules.

feed.title: Xenia Hotels & Resorts stock hits 52-week high at $21.50
feed.summary: Xenia Hotels & Resorts Inc. (XHR) stock has reached a new 52-week high of $21.50, reflecting a significant 61.36% increase over the past year. This achievement follows strong first-quarter 2026 results where the company exceeded earnings and revenue expectations, reporting an EPS of $0.21 against a forecast of $0.17 and revenues of $295.41 million compared to an anticipated $288.4 million. Despite the positive financial performance, the stock experienced a decline in pre-market trading, highlighting unpredictable market reactions.

feed.title: Newmont (NEM) Reports Strong Q2 Earnings, Revenue Surges 15% Yea
feed.summary: Newmont Corp (NEM) reported strong Q2 earnings, with revenue increasing 15% year-over-year to $6.12 billion and profit reaching $2.2 billion, exceeding analyst expectations. The company's GF Score™ is 85/100, indicating robust financial health and growth potential, despite "Significantly Overvalued" valuation and $3.1 million in insider selling activity.

feed.title: Hims & Hers Health, Repligen, West Pharmaceutical Services, and 10x Genomics Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several healthcare-related stocks, including Hims & Hers Health, Repligen, West Pharmaceutical Services, and 10x Genomics, declined after HCA Healthcare's earnings report highlighted a negative payer mix shift due to an increase in uninsured patients. This trend suggests potential profit margin pressure for the broader hospital industry. The article details the individual stock movements and highlights a significant past event for Hims & Hers Health regarding its collaboration with Novo Nordisk.

feed.title: TD Cowen raises Union Pacific stock price target on strong Q2, merger progress
feed.summary: TD Cowen increased its price target for Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) to $325 from $282, maintaining a Buy rating, following the railroad operator's strong second-quarter performance and increased earnings guidance. The company also made progress in addressing shipper optionality and is expected to file further details with the Surface Transportation Board. This positive outlook is further supported by upward revisions in earnings estimates by 16 analysts and impressive gross profit margins of 56%.

feed.title: Align Unveils New Digital Dentistry Innovations
feed.summary: Align Technology has introduced new innovations within its Align Digital Platform, designed to enhance the visualization, planning, and treatment aspects of digital dentistry. These updates include improved patient communication tools, more precise treatment planning capabilities for Invisalign cases, and streamlined workflows for both orthodontic and restorative procedures. The goal is to provide doctors with greater confidence and predictability, enabling them to treat a wider range of cases and improve overall patient experience.

feed.title: Micron, onsemi, Seagate, Western Digital, and Vishay Intertechnology Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Micron, onsemi, Seagate, Western Digital, and Vishay Intertechnology experienced a decline following the U.S. government's announcement of new tariffs (10-12.5%) on 60 trading partners, including key semiconductor supply chain nations like the EU, Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan, due to forced labor concerns. These tariffs are expected to cause long-term margin compression in the U.S. hardware and semiconductor sector. Despite the current sell-off, Micron had previously seen a significant gain fueled by dip-buying and continued optimism in the AI investment cycle.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) Shares Gap Down - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ:CHRW) shares experienced a significant gap down, falling approximately 7.3% at market open. Despite this, Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with a price target of $199.46. The company recently reported earnings per share above estimates, though revenue slightly missed, and it offers a quarterly dividend of $0.63 per share.

feed.title: Revvity Developing Automated Platform for Tuberculosis Testing
feed.summary: Revvity is developing the T-SPOT A201, an automated platform for high-volume latent tuberculosis testing, with a planned launch in the second half of 2027 pending regulatory approval. This system is designed for large clinical laboratories to manage high sample volumes efficiently, reducing hands-on time per sample. The platform aims to improve the accessibility and efficiency of T-SPOT.TB testing at scale.

feed.title: Textron to Post Q2 Earnings: What's in the Cards for the Stock?
feed.summary: Textron Inc. (TXT) is scheduled to release its second-quarter 2026 results on July 28. The company's Q2 revenues are estimated to be $3.82 billion, a 2.8% increase from the prior year, while earnings are projected to be $1.52 per share, a 1.9% decline. Factors like higher aircraft and aftermarket revenues, along with increased sales in the Bell and Textron Systems units, are expected to boost performance, though higher costs could impact the bottom line.

feed.title: TSLP ETF Profile: Dividends, Returns (BOATS:TSLP)
feed.summary: The TSLP ETF, managed by Kurv Investment, Inc., aims to generate monthly income and provide exposure to Tesla (TSLA) stock price returns, capped on potential gains, using a synthetic covered call strategy. The fund has AUM of $19.14M USD, a dividend yield of 38.13%, and an expense ratio of 1.00%. TSLP primarily invests in bonds, with its top holdings being United States Treasury Bills.

feed.title: WIT|Wipro Ltd|Price:1.825|Chg%:+0.045
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of Wipro Ltd (WIT), a technology services and consulting company. It details Wipro's segments (IT Services and IT Products), its services, and key company information such as its CEO, COO, and board members. The current stock price and recent performance are also mentioned.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: World Acceptance beats Q1 2026 EPS forecast
feed.summary: World Acceptance Corp. reported significantly better-than-expected adjusted EPS of $2.12 for Q1 fiscal 2027, surpassing the analyst estimate of $0.44, despite revenue slightly missing forecasts. The company's stock jumped in premarket trading due to improved credit quality, lower provision expense, and higher yields. Management cited disciplined execution in lending, pricing, and credit management, and noted improvements in annualized net charge-offs and front-end delinquency.

feed.title: Chip design complexity drives Synopsys co-design approach in physical AI
feed.summary: Synopsys is addressing the increasing complexity of chip design in the era of physical AI through co-design methodologies and AI-powered agentic workflows. The company's recent acquisition of Ansys enhances its ability to simulate thermal stress while concurrently developing software for un-fabricated hardware. Synopsys also aims to democratize advanced chip design by offering cloud-based tools through a startup program and is exploring quantum computing for future computational challenges.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: SLB tops Q2 2026 estimates as shares jump 9.6%
feed.summary: SLB (NYSE: SLB) surpassed Q2 2026 earnings and revenue estimates, leading to a 9.6% jump in shares during premarket trading. The oilfield services giant reported $0.55 EPS on $8.97 billion in revenue, exceeding analyst expectations of $0.51 EPS and $8.67 billion revenue, driven by strong international performance and margin expansion despite Middle East disruptions. The company's outlook anticipates revenue to surpass $10 billion in Q4, supported by a gradual Middle East recovery and growth in deepwater and digital solutions.

feed.title: KY utilities rush to sign Trump’s data center ratepayer protection pledge
feed.summary: All four investor-owned power companies in Kentucky, along with other utilities and the Tennessee Valley Authority, have signed President Donald Trump's Ratepayer Protection Pledge. This pledge aims to ensure that energy-intensive data centers fund their own power infrastructure costs rather than shifting them to residential customers. The initiative comes amidst growing opposition to large-scale data centers in Kentucky and seeks to reassure the public that the AI boom will bring economic benefits without increasing household electricity bills.

feed.title: Ohio’s Cardinal Health company acquires two medical supply companies for $360 million
feed.summary: Cardinal Health, based in Dublin, Ohio, has acquired AdaptHealth Corp.’s Diabetes Health business and Strive Medical for a combined $360 million. This acquisition will add over 245,000 patients to Cardinal Health’s at-Home Solutions platform, expanding its direct-to-patient healthcare services, particularly in diabetes management and urology. The company anticipates these deals will boost its adjusted earnings per share within the first year after closing.

feed.title: NetApp’s DataPelago Buy Targets The Next Big AI Storage Bottleneck
feed.summary: NetApp has acquired AI infrastructure startup DataPelago to enhance its storage portfolio with GPU and CPU-accelerated data processing capabilities. This acquisition aims to help customers more easily find and utilize data for AI workloads, addressing a critical bottleneck in AI storage. DataPelago's technology, particularly its DataPelago Accelerator for Spark, is designed to significantly improve Apache Spark performance and reduce costs for qualifying workloads without requiring application rewrites or data migration.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: State Street + NYSE to Celebrate 'Fearless Girl'
feed.summary: The NYSE is providing a pre-market update, highlighting escalating tensions in the Middle East affecting oil prices and Vertical Aerospace's recent achievements. Additionally, the NYSE and State Street are co-hosting a summer block party to celebrate 'Fearless Girl', with Opening and Closing Bell ceremonies also planned.

feed.title: Insider Activity Update: Ronald W Tysoe Executes Options Exercise, Resulting In $889K At Cintas
feed.summary: Ronald W Tysoe, a Director at Cintas (NASDAQ: CTAS), exercised stock options for 5,048 shares valued at $889,710 on July 23, 2026. This transaction comes as Cintas shows positive financial trends with a 2.24% revenue growth and a strong gross margin of 50.95%, despite a higher debt-to-equity ratio compared to industry averages. Insider transactions, while not the sole investment factor, provide insights into a company's prospects.

feed.title: Ken’s Foods Partners with Rockwell Automation to Optimize End-of-Line Operations
feed.summary: Ken's Foods has partnered with Rockwell Automation to modernize its end-of-line operations, aiming to improve performance, reduce complexity, and support continued growth. By implementing on-machine architecture with Rockwell Automation PowerFlex 350 VFDs, the company achieved increased visibility, simplified maintenance, and reduced complexity, leading to an estimated 17% overall cost savings. This successful integration has established on-machine architecture as the standard for Ken's Foods' future pallet-conveying systems due to its efficiency and flexibility.

feed.title: TVA signs Ratepayer Protection Pledge to strengthen affordability rates for customers
feed.summary: The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) has signed the Trump administration’s Ratepayer Protection Pledge, reinforcing its commitment to providing affordable energy. Interim President and CEO Mike Skaggs stated that TVA aims to maintain low energy costs and ensure data center expenses are not passed on to customers, especially as data center load is expected to double by 2030. TVA is currently exploring updates to its rate structure for large loads to support AI and other industry growth while balancing impacts on all customers.

feed.title: Halliburton Co Q2 2026: Revenue $5.714B, EPS $0.64— 10-Q Summary
feed.summary: Halliburton Co (HAL) reported strong second-quarter 2026 results, with revenue reaching $5.714 billion and diluted EPS at $0.64, both showing improvement over the previous year. The company attributed this growth to robust performance in drilling-related businesses and international markets, despite a decline in the Middle East/Asia due to regional conflict. Halliburton also noted strong demand for various drilling and completion tools across multiple regions.

feed.title: Smith & Wesson Adds to M&P15 Line with AXE Pistol & SBR in 300 BLK
feed.summary: Smith & Wesson has expanded its M&P15 line by introducing both a pistol and a short-barreled rifle (SBR) chambered in .300 Blackout. The M&P15 AXE Pistol, designed with suppressed shooting in mind, features Gemtech's GVAC direct impingement gas system to reduce gas exposure. While the pistol has an MSRP of $1,479, the SBR version's price has not yet been publicly released.

feed.title: Report: The Shifting Cruise Trends for 2026
feed.summary: A new survey from Cruiseline.com and Shipmate reveals strong demand for cruise vacations in 2026 and beyond, with 92.8% of respondents having at least one voyage booked. The study highlights evolving traveler preferences, including Royal Caribbean International becoming the top-booked cruise line and increased interest in destinations like the Mediterranean and Transatlantic voyages. Travelers are also considering longer itineraries and new cruise lines such as Margaritaville at Sea.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Universal Insurance tops Q2 2026 forecasts, shares jump
feed.summary: Universal Insurance Holdings exceeded Q2 2026 forecasts, with adjusted earnings of $1.84 per share and revenue of $621.3 million, leading to a 10.43% jump in stock price. The Florida-focused insurer attributed its strong performance to improved underwriting, a lower loss ratio, and favorable claims trends. Management highlighted a 33.2% annualized adjusted return on common equity and expressed confidence in continued profitable growth due to Florida's legislative reforms and rate-adequate business strategies.

feed.title: Digital Realty raises 2026 outlook after record second-quarter Core FFO
feed.summary: Digital Realty (NYSE: DLR) raised its 2026 Core FFO outlook after a strong second quarter, with revenue increasing 29% year over year to $1.92 billion and Core FFO reaching a record $2.65 per diluted share. The company reported a significant backlog of $1.9 billion and expanded its portfolio with fully leased data centers in Northern Virginia and a major land acquisition near Kansas City for future development. These achievements have led to an increased full-year Core FFO outlook, excluding net promote income, to $8.15 to $8.20 per diluted share.

feed.title: J&J, Gilead among recipients of EU drug nods this week (JNJ)
feed.summary: The European Medicines Agency's expert panel endorsed 12 new medicines and eight label expansions this week, impacting major pharmaceutical companies. Johnson & Johnson and Gilead are among the U.S. drugmakers receiving positive recommendations for their products. These approvals could allow J&J to enter the EU plaque psoriasis market and Gilead to secure first-line approval for Trodelvy in triple-negative breast cancer.

feed.title: NextEra Energy beats Q2 EPS estimates, Reaffirms 2026 Guidance - NextEra Energy (NYSE:NEE)
feed.summary: NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) reported stronger-than-expected Q2 adjusted EPS of $1.15, a 9.5% increase year-over-year, while revenue of $7.53 billion missed estimates. The company reaffirmed its FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance and highlighted significant growth in its renewable energy backlog, reaching 35.1 GW. NextEra also provided updates on its proposed merger with Dominion Energy, expecting the acquisition to close within 12 to 18 months, which aims to strengthen energy infrastructure and achieve substantial capital and EPS growth.

feed.title: BlackRock Is (NOT) Wall Street's Designated Adult - As BDEBT Highlights The Problem & The Hypocrisy
feed.summary: This article criticizes BlackRock's Private Credit Fund (BDEBT), arguing that it is unsuitable for individual investors despite its availability to them. The author highlights concerns about the fund's illiquid nature, the complex structure of its borrowers (often acquisition vehicles rather than operational companies), and the recent prorogation of investor withdrawal requests. The piece questions BlackRock's role as a "designated adult" in the financial industry given these issues, particularly when retail investors with moderate incomes are involved.
